body.title: Audit reading pipeline + unblock qwen-only completion of the reading list
body.result: Done per Akien 2026-05-03: qwen-2.5:7b confirmed as the winner. No further audit needed.
body.description:
Review Igor's reading/book-learner pipeline end-to-end. Fix whatever is in the way of finishing the entire reading list with local qwen (no cloud OR for extraction). Also make sure every memory deposited from reading carries a well-documented source path (book, chapter, section/page, offset) so we can trace any learned concept back to its origin.

## Why now
- Akien wants to see Igor thoroughly understand his own code. Reading is the main intake for that understanding.
- Cost discipline: extraction should run on local qwen, not OR. D359 (reading-extract-via-gateway) + D360 (7-pass benchmark) are the architectural prerequisites.
- Visibility: when we look at a learned concept and ask 'where did this come from?', the answer should be a crisp path, not 'somewhere in the reading traces'.
- Source-path rigor is also cheap groundwork for later provenance features (citation, contradiction detection, etc.).

## Scope
### Part 1 — Pipeline audit
- Walk the reading code: book_learner, reading_tool, _deposit_nodes, whatever posts to cortex
- Check what's hardcoded to OR vs gateway-routable (D359 not yet done — this ticket may need to do it)
- Identify any choke points: rate limits, prompt sizes, timeout windows, model-specific quirks
- Confirm the reading list and its current progress state

### Part 2 — Qwen readiness
- Run a small chapter through qwen2.5:7b end-to-end and compare output to existing OR-extracted nodes
- Validate: does qwen hit minimum quality bar for concept extraction + relationship inference?
- If not, file a follow-up ticket with specific quality gaps — do NOT hack prompts silently
- If yes, flip the relevant switches (env vars / config) to route reading through local

### Part 3 — Source-path documentation
- Every memory deposited from reading should carry:
  - book_id (canonical name or path on disk)
  - chapter or section identifier
  - page or chunk offset (however the chunker tracks position)
  - extraction_timestamp + extractor_model (so we know WHO said this WHEN)
- Stored in the memory's metadata JSON, not folded into narrative text
- Verify via a spot query: pick 5 random recently-read nodes, walk the source path back to the actual paragraph in the book

## Out of scope
- Adding new books to the list (separate concern)
- Training a custom reading model (D360 tracks benchmark)
- UI for browsing reading traces (visibility separate)

## Success criteria
- Pick a book currently in the list, run it through the pipeline with local qwen as the extractor, watch it complete without failures
- Spot-check 5 resulting memories — each has full source path in metadata
- Reading progress indicator moves forward (no stall)
- No OR calls for extraction during the run (verify via forensic_logger or gateway trace)

## Related
- D359 (reading-extract-via-gateway, planned) — gateway swap for reading
- D360 (reading-model-benchmark, planned) — 7-pass quality benchmark
- D356 (reading-lever-detection, done) — attractor-guided chunk scoring
- GH-252 Research corpus (broader, parent)
- T-neuroscience-engrams (downstream — self-model engrams from AI/ML+neuro books)
- T-foreground-reading-emotional-read (orthogonal — emotional mode)

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/reading/book_learner.py (audit + qwen routing); wild_igor/igor/tools/reading_tool.py (deposit path + source-path metadata); wild_igor/igor/cognition/_deposit_nodes (or wherever cortex-deposit lives — discovery step); wild_igor/igor/inference/gateway.py (D359 — gateway routing for extraction); reading list config (path TBD).

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (reading pipeline is load-bearing); test-plan-or-why-not; budget-respect (qwen-only path = no OR cost).

**Scope boundary:** IN — pipeline audit, qwen-only readiness, source-path metadata on every deposited node, finish reading list. OUT — concept-quality benchmarking beyond minimum bar (Part 2 below); citation/contradiction detection (later provenance work).

**Test plan:** tests/test_reading_pipeline_qwen.py — small chapter end-to-end on qwen2.5:7b, assert non-empty deposit, source-path present (book, chapter, section/page, offset). Comparison test against one OR-extracted reference chunk for sanity (skip in CI without OR cred).
